description: Navamsa (D9) chart rules and deterministic pada→navamsa-sign mapping

concept.name: Navamsa / D9
concept.note: Each sign (30°) is divided into 9 amsas of 3°20' — identical in width to one nakshatra pada. This is why D9 and Nakshatra padas are perfectly aligned.
uses: Confirmation of planetary strength (D1 strength may not hold in D9)

calculation_rule.description: The starting navamsa sign of each rasi depends on the rasi's element (fire/earth/air/water) via the 'Chara-Sthira-Dwi' rotation.
algorithm: 1. For each 3°20' amsa within a sign, the navamsa sign advances by 1 from a starting sign.
2. Starting sign of the 1st amsa of each rasi:
- Fire signs (Aries, Leo, Sagittarius) start at Aries
- Earth signs (Taurus, Virgo, Capricorn) start at Capricorn
- Air signs (Gemini, Libra, Aquarius) start at Libra
- Water signs (Cancer, Scorpio, Pisces) start at Cancer
3. From the start, the 9 amsas progress through the 9 consecutive signs.

vargottama.definition: When a planet or Lagna occupies the same sign in both D1 (Rasi) and D9 (Navamsa).
vargottama.effect: Extra strength — the planet is firmly established in its sign.

vargottama_ranges.note: Vargottama occurs in specific degree bands where navamsa sign equals rasi sign.

pushkara_navamsa.definition: Auspicious specific degrees. A planet placed at Pushkara gives very auspicious results.
pushkara_navamsa.note: Traditionally specific amsas within each sign — refer to classical texts for precise Pushkara degree bands.

karakamsha.definition: Jaimini: the sign in the D9 Navamsa chart occupied by the Atmakaraka.
karakamsha.use: The 12th house from Karakamsha Lagna reveals Ishta Devata (presiding deity) and spiritual path.
karakamsha.atmakaraka_rule: The planet with the highest absolute longitude (ignoring the sign) in the chart. 7 or 8 chara karakas depending on school.

pada_to_navamsa_mapping.description: Each of the 108 nakshatra padas maps to exactly one navamsa sign. Derived from the Nakshatra CSV.
pada_to_navamsa_mapping.rule: The sequence Ashwini Pd1 → Revati Pd4 cycles through the 12 navamsa signs starting from Aries. Every nakshatra spans 4 padas = 4 navamsas.
